normalized_title: Planning Bulletin 114/2024: Residential Design Codes Volume 1 and 2 - Deferred Gazettal, Special Transition Period and Relationship with Pre-Existing Local Planning Frameworks
scope_summary: Explains which version of the Residential Design Codes applies to development applications and approval-exempt development during the 'deferred gazettal period' (R-Codes Vol.1 2021 / Vol.2 2019 until gazettal of the 2024 codes on 10 April 2024) and the 24-month 'special transition period' ending 10 April 2026. It also sets the WAPC's position on how pre-existing local planning instruments (local planning policies, local development plans, structure plans, precinct plans and schemes) that modify deemed-to-comply provisions interact with the R-Codes Vol.1 2024, including the replacement of multiple-dwelling plot ratio with average site area per dwelling, and provides a clause conversion table from R-Codes Vol.1 2021 to Vol.1 2024 Part C.

cross_references.cited: Residential Design Codes Volume 1 (R-Codes Vol.1 2021 and 2024)
Residential Design Codes Volume 2 (R-Codes Vol.2 2019 and 2023/2024)
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015 (deemed provisions, cl 61, cl 67(2), cl 75, r.66)

quality_flags: summary.json version_date of '2 July 2021' is wrong - that is the gazettal date of R-Codes Vol.1 2021 quoted in the bulletin's definitions; the bulletin itself was published 8 March 2024

The special transition period and special transitional deemed-to-comply provisions described ceased to have effect on 10 April 2026 (before the corpus check date of 2026-06-10), so the transitional arrangements are now largely spent; the guidance on local planning instrument alignment with R-Codes Vol.1 2024 remains relevant
Multi-column PDF interleaving scrambles sentence order in places; Appendix 1 conversion table extracted cleanly
